title: Item 6 — Other Fees
rows: Paid weekly. We may propose to increase up to 7% (requires majority approval). Also applies to delivery and catering.
May be increased up to 6% with majority system vote. FY2025 combined marketing/local was 4.0%; increased to 4.5% effective 9/29/2025.
May be modified with 90 days notice.
Payable at signing of successor FA.
If underreported by 3%+ of Gross Sales, pay cost of audit plus interest on underpayment.
Lesser of 1.5%/month or max legal rate
Must maintain required coverages; if you fail, we may obtain and you reimburse.
Only for training beyond DO, GM, and 2 leaders.
DO, GM, and/or leaders must attend as required.
First two franchised restaurants no charge.
If restaurant fails food safety or operational inspection.
Must indemnify us for claims arising from your operations.
If FA terminated early, damages may include lost royalties for remainder of term.
table_notes: All fees are non-refundable. Gross Sales is defined in the Franchise Agreement and includes all revenue from the restaurant including delivery and catering, less taxes, promotional discounts, and complimentary items. EFT = electronic funds transfer via ACH prearranged payment authorization. Royalty rate may be increased to maximum 7% with franchisee vote. Marketing fund rate was 4.0% during FY2025 and increased to 4.5% on September 29, 2025.

title: Item 7 — Estimated Initial Investment (Traditional Qdoba Restaurant)
table_notes: Amounts include $40,000 payable to franchisor or affiliates. Estimates based on franchisor's experience. Actual costs may vary based on location, local costs, and market conditions. Additional funds cover initial operating costs for first 3 months; does not include owner's salary or living expenses. Non-traditional investment range is $234,500 to $898,000 (franchise fee $20,000). Under Development Agreement (1 restaurant): $549,100 to $1,299,000 (includes $10,000 development fee).

title: Item 19 Chart 1 — Overall Franchise Sales Growth (390 restaurants, 3-year same-store)
table_notes: Excluded: 13 Canadian, 123 non-traditional, 5 refranchised, 38 newly opened, 81 closed >21 days. Data based on unaudited POS sales. FY2023: median $1,476,793, high $4,708,597, low $431,709. FY2024: median $1,607,864, high $5,492,729, low $417,422. FY2025: median $1,647,607, high $5,078,990, low $422,629.

title: Item 19 Chart 2 — Franchised Restaurant Quartile Net Sales (464 restaurants, TTM ended 9/28/2025)
table_notes: Included 464 franchised restaurants open 1+ year as of 9/28/2025. Excluded: 123 non-traditional, 5 refranchised, 38 newly opened, 13 Canadian, 7 closed 21+ days. Data based on unaudited POS sales.

title: Item 19 Chart 3 — Historical Average Sales, Selected Costs and Operating Results (397 restaurants)
table_notes: (1) Net Restaurant Sales = all food, beverage, and catering sales, excluding sales/service taxes less promotion costs (coupons, promotional discounts, manager-complimentary items).
(2) Restaurant Cost of Sales section excludes depreciation and amortization expenses.
(3) Cost of sales includes food, paper, packaging costs, net of vendor rebates. May vary by geography.
(4) Salaries and benefits include restaurant management wages (not above-store), shift supervisors, management bonuses, payroll taxes, group insurance, workers comp, vacation, other benefits.

(5) Other operating expenses include repairs/maintenance, smallwares, cleaning, office supplies, POS maintenance, pest control, delivery charges, credit card processing, bank charges, telephone/internet, trash, equipment rental, property/liability insurance, security, licenses/taxes, utilities, miscellaneous.

(6) Occupancy costs include base rent, percentage rent, CAM, real estate taxes, personal property taxes, landlord insurance, miscellaneous lease expenses. 3 restaurants had no reported lease expense (purchased real estate); average 7.5% used for those.
(7) Royalty fee defined in Item 6. Advertising fees reflect combined Marketing Fee and Local Advertising of 4.0% for FY ended 9/28/2025. Increased to 4.5% on 9/29/2025.
(8) Total Cost of Revenues does not include development fees, franchise fees, and administrative costs.

Included 397 traditional franchised restaurants reporting via online portal for trailing 12-month periods (varying end dates). Excluded: 128 non-traditional, 5 refranchised, 38 newly opened, 13 Canadian, 7 closed 21+ days, 64 not yet reporting.

title: Item 20 Table 1 — Systemwide Outlet Summary (FY 2023–2025)
table_notes: Significant refranchising in FY2023: 110 company-owned restaurants sold to franchisees. FY2024: 11 sold. FY2025: 3 sold. System has shifted from ~39% franchised in early 2023 to ~79% franchised by end of FY2025.

title: Item 20 Table 2 — Transfers of Outlets from Franchisees to New Owners (FY 2023–2025)
table_notes: FY2025 saw increase in transfers vs prior years, driven by North Carolina (9 transfers).

title: Item 20 Table 3 — Status of Franchised Outlets (FY 2023–2025, state-by-state)
table_notes: Full state-by-state detail available across pages 64-68. During 2023, 110 company-owned restaurants were sold to franchisees. During 2024, 11 in Maryland. During 2025, 3 (2 in Missouri, 1 in Kansas). Stars (*) denote states that received refranchised units. 0 terminations across all 3 years. Reacquired by franchisor: 0 in 2023-2024, 8 in 2025 (4 in Florida, 4 in New Jersey).
reason_if_partial: Summary totals extracted; full state-by-state rows available in PDF pages 64-68 but not all reproduced here to manage volume. 40+ states represented.

title: Item 20 Table 4 — Status of Company-Owned Outlets (FY 2023–2025)
table_notes: FY2023: massive refranchising wave (110 sold including 25 in Indiana, 23 in Kentucky, 22 in Missouri, 17 in Washington, 8 in Colorado, etc.). FY2025: company reacquired 8 from franchisees (4 FL, 4 NJ) while also opening 9 new company units and selling 3. Michigan (51) and Colorado (60) remain largest company-owned state concentrations.
reason_if_partial: Summary totals extracted; state-by-state detail available in PDF pages 68-71.

title: Item 20 Table 5 — Projected Openings (as of September 28, 2025)
column_headers: Projected New Franchised Outlets Next FY
Projected New Company-Owned Outlets Next FY
table_notes: 77 projected new franchised outlets and 12 new company-owned outlets in next fiscal year. 14 signed-but-not-open franchise agreements. Largest projected franchise openings: Ohio (9), New York (7), New Jersey (6), Florida (5). Company-owned projected: Michigan (4), Arizona (3), Illinois (3), Massachusetts (2). International projected: Japan (3 locations across Aomori, Okinawa, Tokyo).

title: Item 11 Training Table A — Team Member / Shift Lead / General Manager / Designated Operator
table_notes: TM=Team Member, SL=Shift Lead, GM=General Manager, DO=Designated Operator. * = If required by state/local law. ** = Required if not already certified with accredited Food Safety organization; franchisee pays. Training is proficiency-based, not time-based; actual hours may vary.

title: Item 11 Training Table B — Manager in Training
table_notes: MIT = Manager in Training. DO = Designated Operator. Proficiency-based training; hours are estimates. New training program to be introduced next fiscal year for core team members and shift leads.

title: Item 17 — Renewal, Termination, Transfer and Dispute Resolution Summary
rows: 1 renewal for 10 years if conditions met
6-18 months notice, not in default, current standards, then-current FA, $10,000 fee, remodel if required, general release
Immediate or with 30/60-day cure depending on nature of default
With consent, up to $5,000 fee, transferee qualifications, then-current FA, training, release, ROFR
During term + 2 years post-term/transfer within 5 miles of any Qdoba
Nonbinding mediation, then litigation in San Diego, CA. Subject to state law.
table_notes: Full Item 17 table spans pages 50-58 with detailed provisions for FA, License Agreement, and Development Agreement. Key risk: post-term noncompete extends 5 miles from ANY Qdoba restaurant, not just the franchisee's former location. State addenda may modify certain provisions.
reason_if_partial: Summary-level extraction of key provisions. Full tabular detail across FA, LA, and DA covers pages 50-58.
